ogTitle: Burnett Heads vs Burpengary: Climate Safety

heading: Burnett Heads vs Burpengary — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burnett Heads, Queensland and Burpengary, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burnett Heads has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Burpengary (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burnett Heads or Burpengary safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burnett Heads has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Burpengary scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burnett Heads scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Burpengary scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burnett Heads or Burpengary?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burnett Heads has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
